definition: The 'compressed spring' condition (extreme DM rate divergence plus suppressed FX vol) is associated with materially elevated probability of a 20%+ G10 pair move within 12 months. The directional finding is robust (CI lower bound of ~26-36% is 2.5-3.3x the unconditional 11%) but the specific 61% point estimate is inflated by small-sample inference (n≈8-12 effective independent episodes, 95% CI [26%, 88%]).
evidence: Validated through two independent methods: statistical base rate and historical episode sequencing
August 2024 out-of-sample episode provides partial validation
Debate pair_0: 'the highest-conviction finding across both analyses'
Even CI lower bound of ~26-36% is 2.5-3.3x the unconditional 11% base rate
open_questions: Whether the reference distribution for 'extreme' divergence is stationary (KD5: 1990-2010 convergence may have been the anomaly)
Whether the compressed spring is at the 60th or 97th percentile depending on reference period choice
Resolution of p-value under proper multiple-testing correction
